Name: API-AUTH-001 - Create a payment authorization with a valid card
Objective: Verify that create a payment authorization with a valid card.
Precondition: Merchant has an active API key with payments:write scope; sandbox environment reachable.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments with a valid card and amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the response body.
Expected Result: Response includes payment_id and status=AUTHORIZED.

Step: 3. Verify the card representation in the response.
Expected Result: Card is returned masked as **** 1111; no raw PAN appears anywhere in the payload.

Name: API-AUTH-002 - Authorization fails gracefully for a declined test card
Objective: Verify that authorization fails gracefully for a declined test card.
Precondition: Sandbox decline-trigger card is configured; merchant API key is valid.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments using the sandbox decline-trigger card.
Expected Result: Response status is 200 or 402 (per API contract), not a 5xx error.

Step: 2. Inspect the payment status and decline reason.
Expected Result: status=DECLINED and a decline_reason code is present.

Step: 3. Query the payment afterward via GET.
Expected Result: No payment record is left in an ambiguous or partially-authorized state.

Name: API-AUTH-003 - Full capture of a previously authorized payment
Objective: Verify that full capture of a previously authorized payment.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=AUTHORIZED and amount=5000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/capture for the full authorized amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the resulting payment status.
Expected Result: status=CAPTURED and captured_amount equals the authorized amount.

Step: 3. Confirm downstream handling.
Expected Result: Funds move into the settlement pipeline.

Name: API-AUTH-004 - Partial capture of an authorized payment
Objective: Verify that partial capture of an authorized payment.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=AUTHORIZED and amount=5000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/capture for less than the full authorized amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the resulting payment status and captured_amount.
Expected Result: status=PARTIALLY_CAPTURED and captured_amount=2000.

Step: 3. Check the remaining authorized balance.
Expected Result: Remaining balance (3000) is released or held per policy, consistently reflected in the response.

Name: API-AUTH-005 - Capture amount exceeding the authorized amount is rejected
Objective: Verify that capture amount exceeding the authorized amount is rejected.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=AUTHORIZED and amount=5000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/capture requesting more than the authorized amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_AUTHORIZATION is present.

Step: 3. Verify no capture was recorded.
Expected Result: GET on the payment shows no capture entry and status remains AUTHORIZED.

Name: API-AUTH-006 - Capturing an already-captured payment is rejected
Objective: Verify that capturing an already-captured payment is rejected.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=CAPTURED.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/capture again against an already-captured payment.
Expected Result: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION is present.

Step: 3. Verify the original capture record.
Expected Result: Original capture record is unchanged.

Name: API-AUTH-007 - Void an authorized (uncaptured) payment
Objective: Verify that void an authorized (uncaptured) payment.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=AUTHORIZED.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/void with a cancellation reason.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the resulting status.
Expected Result: status=VOIDED.

Step: 3. Verify the held funds.
Expected Result: Held funds are released back to the cardholder's available balance.

Name: API-AUTH-008 - Void is rejected once a payment has been captured
Objective: Verify that void is rejected once a payment has been captured.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=CAPTURED.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/void against a captured payment.
Expected Result: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error message.
Expected Result: Error message directs the caller to use /refund instead.

Step: 3. Verify state is unchanged.
Expected Result: Payment status remains CAPTURED; no state change occurs.

Name: API-REF-001 - Full refund of a captured payment
Objective: Verify that full refund of a captured payment.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=CAPTURED and amount=5000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/refund for the full captured amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the resulting status.
Expected Result: status=REFUNDED and a refund_id is returned.

Step: 3. Verify refunded_amount.
Expected Result: refunded_amount equals the captured amount.

Name: API-REF-002 - Partial refund of a captured payment
Objective: Verify that partial refund of a captured payment.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=CAPTURED and amount=5000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/refund for less than the captured amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the resulting status.
Expected Result: status=PARTIALLY_REFUNDED and refundable balance decreases to 3500.

Step: 3. Issue a second partial refund within the remaining balance.
Expected Result: Second partial refund succeeds; multiple partial refunds are allowed up to the captured total.

Name: API-REF-003 - Refund exceeding the remaining refundable balance is rejected
Objective: Verify that refund exceeding the remaining refundable balance is rejected.
Precondition: A payment was previously partially refunded 3500 of 5000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/refund for more than the remaining refundable balance.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code REFUND_EXCEEDS_BALANCE is present.

Step: 3. Verify no refund record was created.
Expected Result: GET on the payment shows the refundable balance unchanged at 1500.

Name: API-REF-004 - Refund on a payment with no capture (AUTHORIZED only) is rejected
Objective: Verify that refund on a payment with no capture (AUTHORIZED only) is rejected.
Precondition: A payment exists with status=AUTHORIZED.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments/{id}/refund against an authorized-only payment.
Expected Result: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION is present, with guidance to void instead.

Name: API-TOK-001 - Tokenize a card for future use
Objective: Verify that tokenize a card for future use.
Precondition: Merchant has tokens:write scope; valid card details are supplied.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payment-methods with card details.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the response body.
Expected Result: A non-reversible token_id is returned.

Step: 3. Check logs and the response for raw PAN exposure.
Expected Result: Raw PAN is never echoed back or persisted in plaintext logs.

Name: API-TOK-002 - Charge a saved token for a recurring subscription payment
Objective: Verify that charge a saved token for a recurring subscription payment.
Precondition: A valid, non-expired token_id exists for the merchant's customer.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments referencing the saved token, without card entry fields.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the resulting status.
Expected Result: status=CAPTURED (or AUTHORIZED per configured flow).

Name: API-TOK-003 - Charging an expired or revoked token is rejected
Objective: Verify that charging an expired or revoked token is rejected.
Precondition: The token was previously deleted via DELETE /v1/payment-methods/{id}.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments referencing the revoked token.
Expected Result: HTTP 410 Gone or 422 is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code TOKEN_INVALID is present.

Step: 3. Confirm no charge reached the card network.
Expected Result: No downstream charge attempt is recorded against the card network.

Name: API-TOK-004 - Revoke a stored token
Objective: Verify that revoke a stored token.
Precondition: The token exists and is active.
Step: 1. Send DELETE /v1/payment-methods/{id} for the token.
Expected Result: HTTP 204 No Content is returned.

Step: 2. Attempt a subsequent charge using the same token.
Expected Result: The charge attempt fails with error code TOKEN_INVALID.

Name: API-WHK-001 - Successful webhook delivery on payment status change
Objective: Verify that successful webhook delivery on payment status change.
Precondition: Merchant has a registered, reachable webhook endpoint.
Step: 1. Trigger an event that changes payment status.
Expected Result: Webhook is delivered within the SLA window.

Step: 2. Inspect the delivered payload.
Expected Result: Payload includes event type, payment_id, and a signature header.

Step: 3. Confirm the merchant endpoint's response.
Expected Result: Merchant endpoint returns a 2xx status for the delivery.

Name: API-WHK-002 - Webhook retry on merchant endpoint failure
Objective: Verify that webhook retry on merchant endpoint failure.
Precondition: Merchant endpoint is configured to return HTTP 500 on the first two attempts.
Step: 1. Trigger an event with the merchant endpoint failing initially.
Expected Result: NexaPay retries delivery with backoff (e.g., up to 5 attempts over an increasing interval).

Step: 2. Allow the endpoint to keep failing until retries are exhausted.
Expected Result: Delivery is marked failed only after retries are exhausted.

Step: 3. Inspect delivery logs.
Expected Result: Retries are logged with attempt count and timestamps.

Name: API-WHK-003 - Duplicate webhook delivery is safely handled via at-least-once semantics
Objective: Verify that duplicate webhook delivery is safely handled via at-least-once semantics.
Precondition: A webhook for the same event is redelivered due to a delayed acknowledgement.
Step: 1. Trigger delivery of an event and force a redelivery of the same event_id.
Expected Result: Both deliveries include the same, stable event_id.

Step: 2. Confirm documented delivery semantics.
Expected Result: Documentation/behavior confirms at-least-once (not exactly-once) delivery, allowing the merchant to de-duplicate.

Name: API-MER-001 - Create a new merchant application
Objective: Verify that create a new merchant application.
Precondition: Caller has platform-level onboarding credentials.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/merchants with legal and business details.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the response body.
Expected Result: A merchant_id is returned and kyb_status=PENDING.

Name: API-MER-002 - Retrieve KYB (Know-Your-Business) verification status
Objective: Verify that retrieve KYB (Know-Your-Business) verification status.
Precondition: The merchant application exists and has been submitted for review.
Step: 1. Send GET /v1/merchants/{id}/kyb-status.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the response body.
Expected Result: Returns one of PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, or ADDITIONAL_INFO_REQUIRED with a last_updated timestamp.

Name: API-MER-003 - Payment creation is blocked for a merchant whose KYB is not yet approved
Objective: Verify that payment creation is blocked for a merchant whose KYB is not yet approved.
Precondition: A merchant exists with kyb_status=PENDING.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments for the pending merchant.
Expected Result: HTTP 403 Forbidden is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code MERCHANT_NOT_ACTIVATED is present.

Step: 3. Verify no payment record was created.
Expected Result: GET /v1/payments for this merchant shows no new record.

Name: API-IDEM-001 - Duplicate request with the same idempotency key returns the original result
Objective: Verify that duplicate request with the same idempotency key returns the original result.
Precondition: An initial payment request succeeded using Idempotency-Key: idem-001.
Step: 1. Resend an identical payload with the same idempotency key.
Expected Result: HTTP 200/201 is returned with the same payment_id and status as the original call.

Step: 2. Check the card network/ledger for a second charge.
Expected Result: No second charge is created against the card.

Name: API-IDEM-002 - Same idempotency key with a different payload is rejected
Objective: Verify that same idempotency key with a different payload is rejected.
Precondition: An initial payment request succeeded with Idempotency-Key: idem-002, amount=5000.
Step: 1. Resend the same idempotency key with a changed amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_CONFLICT is present.

Step: 3. Verify the original payment.
Expected Result: Original payment record is untouched.

Name: API-IDEM-003 - Concurrent duplicate requests with the same idempotency key create only one charge
Objective: Verify that concurrent duplicate requests with the same idempotency key create only one charge.
Precondition: Two requests share an identical idempotency key and payload.
Step: 1. Fire two requests with the identical key and payload near-simultaneously.
Expected Result: Exactly one payment_id is created.

Step: 2. Compare both responses.
Expected Result: The second response returns the same result as the first, with no race-condition double charge.

Name: API-LIST-001 - List transactions with default pagination
Objective: Verify that list transactions with default pagination.
Precondition: Merchant has more than one page of transactions (e.g., 120 records, default page size 50).
Step: 1. Send GET /v1/payments with no query parameters.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned with the first 50 records.

Step: 2. Inspect the response metadata.
Expected Result: A next_cursor and total count metadata are present.

Name: API-LIST-002 - Filter transactions by status and date range
Objective: Verify that filter transactions by status and date range.
Precondition: Merchant has a mix of AUTHORIZED, CAPTURED, and REFUNDED transactions across several days.
Step: 1. Send GET /v1/payments with status and date-range filters.
Expected Result: Only CAPTURED transactions within the date range are returned.

Step: 2. Retrieve a second page and inspect ordering.
Expected Result: Results are sorted consistently (e.g., newest first) across pages.

Name: API-LIST-003 - Requesting a page beyond the last cursor returns an empty result, not an error
Objective: Verify that requesting a page beyond the last cursor returns an empty result, not an error.
Precondition: Merchant has exactly 2 pages of results.
Step: 1. Send GET /v1/payments using a cursor value past the last page.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the response body.
Expected Result: An empty data array and next_cursor=null are returned; no 4xx/5xx error occurs.

Name: API-ERR-001 - Invalid API key is rejected
Objective: Verify that invalid API key is rejected.
Precondition: No valid API key is supplied, or a revoked key is used.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments with an invalid bearer token.
Expected Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code INVALID_API_KEY is present with only a generic message (no key-guessing hints).

Name: API-ERR-002 - Malformed JSON payload is rejected with a clear error
Objective: Verify that malformed JSON payload is rejected with a clear error.
Precondition: A well-formed API key is used; the payload is syntactically invalid JSON.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments with syntactically invalid JSON.
Expected Result: HTTP 400 Bad Request is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code MALFORMED_REQUEST is present; no server stack trace is leaked in the response body.

Name: API-ERR-003 - Unsupported currency code is rejected
Objective: Verify that unsupported currency code is rejected.
Precondition: A valid API key is used and the payload is otherwise complete.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/payments with a non-ISO-4217 currency code.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code UNSUPPORTED_CURRENCY is present, listing supported currencies or a documentation link.

Name: API-ERR-004 - API key with insufficient scope is rejected
Objective: Verify that aPI key with insufficient scope is rejected.
Precondition: The API key only has payments:read scope, not merchants:write.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/merchants using a payments-scoped key.
Test Data: Attempt to create a merchant with a payments-scoped key
Expected Result: HTTP 403 Forbidden is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE is present, naming the required scope.

Name: API-WALLET-001 - Initiate a wallet top-up from a linked bank card
Objective: Verify that initiate a wallet top-up from a linked bank card.
Precondition: Consumer has an active NexaPay Wallet and a linked verified bank card.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/topups with a linked card as the funding source.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the response body.
Expected Result: Response includes topup_id and status=PENDING (or SUCCESS for synchronous card funding).

Step: 3. Check the wallet balance before the funding source confirms.
Expected Result: Wallet balance is not credited until the funding source confirms success.

Name: API-WALLET-002 - Wallet top-up via bank transfer settles asynchronously
Objective: Verify that wallet top-up via bank transfer settles asynchronously.
Precondition: Consumer has generated a unique bank-transfer reference for funding.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/topups with source=bank_transfer and a reference.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned with status=PENDING.

Step: 2. Check wallet balance prior to the matching webhook.
Expected Result: Wallet balance is unchanged until the matching bank-transfer webhook confirms receipt.

Step: 3. Deliver the confirming webhook and re-check the balance.
Expected Result: Wallet balance is credited only after the webhook confirms success.

Name: API-WALLET-003 - Wallet top-up fails gracefully when the linked card is declined
Objective: Verify that wallet top-up fails gracefully when the linked card is declined.
Precondition: The linked card is a sandbox decline-trigger card.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/topups using the sandbox decline-trigger card.
Expected Result: Response status is 200 or 402 with status=FAILED and a decline_reason code.

Step: 2. Check the wallet balance after the decline.
Expected Result: Wallet balance is unaffected; no partial credit is applied.

Name: API-P2P-001 - Successful P2P transfer to another NexaPay user by phone number
Objective: Verify that successful P2P transfer to another NexaPay user by phone number.
Precondition: Sender wallet has sufficient balance; recipient is an active NexaPay Wallet user.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/transfers with a recipient phone number and amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned with status=COMPLETED.

Step: 2. Inspect both wallet balances after the transfer.
Expected Result: Sender balance decreases by 5000 and recipient balance increases by 5000 in the same logical operation.

Name: API-P2P-002 - P2P transfer is rejected when the sender has insufficient balance
Objective: Verify that p2P transfer is rejected when the sender has insufficient balance.
Precondition: Sender wallet balance is 3000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/transfers requesting more than the available balance.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload and both balances.
Expected Result: Error code INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE is present; neither balance changes.

Name: API-P2P-003 - Self-transfer is blocked
Objective: Verify that self-transfer is blocked.
Precondition: Sender attempts to transfer to their own wallet/username.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/transfers with the sender's own wallet as the recipient.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code SELF_TRANSFER_NOT_ALLOWED is present; no transfer record is created.

Name: API-P2P-004 - Per-transaction transfer limit is enforced
Objective: Verify that per-transaction transfer limit is enforced.
Precondition: Sender's configured per-transaction P2P limit is AED 10000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/transfers exceeding the per-transaction limit.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload.
Expected Result: Error code TRANSFER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED is present, indicating the applicable per-transaction limit.

Name: API-P2P-005 - Daily cumulative transfer limit is enforced
Objective: Verify that daily cumulative transfer limit is enforced.
Precondition: Sender has already transferred AED 9000 today against a daily limit of AED 10000.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/transfers that would push the cumulative daily total over the limit.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload and prior transfers.
Expected Result: Error code DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED is present; prior successful transfers for the day are unaffected.

Name: API-BILL-001 - Successful bill payment to a registered biller from wallet balance
Objective: Verify that successful bill payment to a registered biller from wallet balance.
Precondition: Wallet has sufficient balance; biller is registered in the biller directory.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/bill-payments with a valid biller and reference.
Expected Result: HTTP 201 Created is returned with status=PENDING.

Step: 2. Poll until the payment reaches a terminal status.
Expected Result: Status transitions to SUCCESS.

Step: 3. Check the wallet balance.
Expected Result: Wallet balance decreases by the paid amount only once status reaches SUCCESS.

Name: API-BILL-002 - Invalid or malformed biller reference is rejected
Objective: Verify that invalid or malformed biller reference is rejected.
Precondition: Biller requires a reference matching a documented format.
Step: 1. Send POST /v1/wallet/bill-payments with a malformed biller_reference.
Expected Result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload and wallet balance.
Expected Result: Error code INVALID_BILLER_REFERENCE is present; no debit is applied to the wallet.

Name: API-BILL-003 - Duplicate bill payment for the same biller reference within a short window is prevented
Objective: Verify that duplicate bill payment for the same biller reference within a short window is prevented.
Precondition: An identical bill payment for the same biller_reference and amount succeeded moments earlier.
Step: 1. Resend an identical bill payment request for the same biller_reference and amount.
Expected Result: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned.

Step: 2. Inspect the error payload and wallet balance.
Expected Result: Error code DUPLICATE_BILL_PAYMENT is present; wallet is not debited a second time; original payment record is returned for reference.

Name: API-BILL-004 - Bill payment status polling reflects the pending-to-terminal transition
Objective: Verify that bill payment status polling reflects the pending-to-terminal transition.
Precondition: A bill payment was just submitted and is being processed by the biller's system.
Step: 1. Send GET /v1/wallet/bill-payments/{id} immediately after submission.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 OK is returned with status=PENDING.

Step: 2. Poll again after the biller system responds.
Expected Result: Status progresses to either SUCCESS or FAILED; polling never returns an intermediate/undefined status value.
